codeReview.summary: The feature correctly resets bridgeHydrated on live runtime establishment, removes the Dashboard workaround, and adds strong regression coverage. However, the new stateful MockAppStateProvider introduces a behavioral regression: it no longer responds to updated `value` props, so demo scenario switches can carry stale state between scenarios.

issues.description: MockAppStateProvider initializes `profiles/activeProfile/runtimeStatus/signerPaused/createSession` from `value` only once via `useState(...)` and never re-syncs when `value` changes. DemoScenarioPage passes `value={scenario.appState}` based on route params (`src/demo/DemoScenarioPage.tsx:41`), so navigating between `/demo/:scenarioId` entries can retain prior scenario state instead of applying the new scenario's seed state. This is a regression from prior behavior where provider output tracked `value` directly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-builder skill has conflicting guidance for non-UI features: it explicitly allows architecture/provider-only work, but still marks `agent-browser` as a required skill and includes mandatory manual UI verification steps. This mismatch led to a transcript that did not invoke `agent-browser` while handoff feedback still marked procedure as followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/ui-builder/SKILL.md:22 (non-UI features are valid) vs :31-34 (agent-browser required); worker transcript skeleton for session 6296db94-8c4a-4e76-b1c4-bac7355248d2 shows only mission-worker-base + ui-builder skill invocations and no agent-browser call.

summary: Failing review due to one blocking regression: MockAppStateProvider became stateful without syncing prop updates, which can leak stale appState across demo scenario switches. Core bridgeHydrated reset behavior and added regression tests are otherwise implemented well.
